q: What is the Temple of Dakka, an ancient Nubian structure relocated during the construction of the Aswan High Dam?
a: Ancient Egyptian temple
e: The Temple of Dakka is an ancient Egyptian temple originally located in Nubia (now Sudan). It was dismantled and relocated to the Wadi es-Sebua site during the UNESCO rescue operation following construction of the Aswan High Dam in the 1960s.

q: What is Luxor Temple, the ancient Egyptian monument built around 1399 BCE on the east bank of the Nile?
a: Ancient Egyptian temple
e: Luxor Temple is an ancient Egyptian temple complex located on the east bank of the Nile in Luxor, established around 1399 BCE. It was built largely by Amenhotep III and Ramesses II.

q: What is the Temple of Cybele, the ancient Roman temple on the Palatine Hill dedicated on April 11, 191 BCE?

q: What is the Temple of Venus and Roma in Rome — the largest ancient Roman temple — and where was it built between the Forum Romanum and the Colosseum?
a: Temple on the Velian Hill between the Forum Romanum and the Colosseum, dedicated to Venus Felix and Roma Aeterna
e: The Temple of Venus and Roma was the largest ancient Roman temple, located on the Velian Hill between the Forum Romanum and the Colosseum. It was dedicated to Venus Felix ('Venus the Bringer of Good Fortune') and Roma Aeterna ('Eternal Rome'), completed around 135 CE under Hadrian.
i: Rewrote question to highlight largest temple status. Distractors are all legitimate Roman structures — retained.

q: What is Amada — the ancient Egyptian temple complex relocated during the UNESCO Nubia rescue campaign?
a: Archaeological site in Egypt
e: Amada is an archaeological site in Egypt containing an ancient Egyptian temple, one of the oldest in Nubia, built during the reign of Thutmose III around the 15th century BCE. It was relocated during the UNESCO campaign to save Nubian monuments from the Aswan High Dam reservoir.

q: What is the Second Temple of Hera at Paestum, the large ancient structure still standing in southern Italy after 2,500 years?
a: Ancient Greek temple
e: The Second Temple of Hera at Paestum is a large ancient Greek temple in the ancient Greek colony of Paestum (Poseidonia) in southern Italy, built around 460-450 BCE. It is one of the best-preserved ancient Greek temples in the world.

q: What is Jaromarsburg — the ancient Slavic cult site on the island of Rügen that was the center of Rugian tribal religion?
a: Ancient cult location
e: Jaromarsburg (also known as Arkona) is an ancient cult location on the island of Rügen, Germany. It was the religious center of the Rugian Slavic people, home to a famous wooden temple dedicated to the god Svantevit, destroyed by the Danes in 1168.
i: Placeholder distractors replaced. Rewrote question to include Rügen island and Slavic context. Explanation was bare — expanded.
